title: How is the dub of 3010
wikitext:
[]: How is the dub of 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Thu Aug 01, 2024 7:30 pm I'm rewatching all four movies and I'm considering going with the dub for the fourth movie when I get to it, since I haven't seen the dub outside of clips of the rough one that was done for the Amazon Prime release [like where Fuyutsuki is talking to Gendo in the NERV control room and he says the same line twice, they left in a bad take that's a Sonic 06 mistake what the goddamn f-] So how is the dub on the BD release? Did they clean it up from the Amazon Prime version? Is it accurate to the Japanese script? Obviously I've seen 3+1 way fewer times than the other movies so I'm not as familiar with it, I'd hate to watch the dub and pick up on a weirdly translated line that causes me to interpret something incorrectly. Though this IS GKids and not the absolute dipshits at Funimation, the same company who made that original 3.0 dub that was lost media for a while- the one where, when they screened it at a convention, it had the audience laughing [To go off topic for a sec, I sat down and rewatched 3.0 with that dub audio when it was found and it was nowhere near as bad as it had been built up to be. Like yeah it wasn't GOOD but it wasn't a travesty... though I can see why the type of people who would attend an anime convention would be laughing at it ]

[]: Re: How is the dub of 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Aug 03, 2024 3:16 pm The Amazon Prime dub should be the only English dub done for 3.0+1.0. I appreciate the work done by Funimation as they worked on the three previous movies before they went bust and Amazon prime decided to hire the old dub actors from the series to return. So if you like the return of Amanda Winn Lee and John Swasey then it should be ok. Amanda speaks with a slight lisp in her performance for Rei 2. The translations are ok but there only small variations compared with the subtitles, most notably that when Shinji says goodbye Asuka in the end it does not match the timing of the subtitles (should be "I liked you too" *pause* "good bye Asuka give my regards to Kensuke" and not "I liked you too good bye Asuka" *pause* "give my regards to kensuke").

[]: Re: How is the dub of 3.0+1.0?             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Thu Aug 08, 2024 9:56 pm After watching all four movies, I'm just gonna parrot what everyone else has said about the new dubs- a really accurate but too literal translation, with not great voice direction. And yes they left the Fuyutsuki bad take in the Blu Ray release, so nothing was changed between the Amazon Prime digital release and the Blu Ray [it's the line about Unit 13's reactivation only being a matter of time, dude repeats himself like Jacob Two-Two] The better translations paired with being able to fully appreciate the art and animations without having to read subtitles at the same time gave me a deeper understanding of the story, and of course I noticed and picked up on new things I'd missed before, so though I personally prefer the delivery and voice direction of the Funimation dubs [I can't believe I just complimented Funimation], I think the new dubs are the better way to watch the New Movies with English audio. Regardless the ending of 3.0+1.0 still had me bawling, crying like a bitch man, like heavily uncontrollably weeping, the salt in my tears stinging my eyes. Fuck I love 3+1.
